<think>
Okay, let's try to figure out if the code has any of the specified vulnerabilities. The target function is display_record, which allocates memory for a Record struct, fills it with data, and prints it. 

First, checking for CWE-401 (Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime). This occurs when memory isn't freed even after it's no longer needed. The code uses malloc to allocate memory for 'rec' but doesn't call free anywhere. However, since the function doesn't return the pointer or leave it dangling, there's no scenario where the memory isn't released. The function's scope ends after allocation, so the memory leak isn't a CWE-401 issue here.

Next, considering CWE-415 (Double Free). The code doesn't have any free calls, so this isn't applicable. 

For CWE-416 (Use After Free), there's no freeing of memory before using it. The 'rec' pointer is used immediately after allocation, so no use-after-free occurs.

Looking at CWE-787 (Out-of-bounds Write), the fill_record function uses snprintf with sizeof(rec->name), which is 64. The format string "entry_%d" for id=1000 would result in "entry_1000" (10 chars), well within the 64-byte buffer. No overflow occurs here.

The code doesn't exhibit any of the listed vulnerabilities. The memory leak from not freeing 'rec' isn't part of the specified CWEs. Thus, no vulnerabilities are present.
</think>
